user_task: Filter results by document attributes (source, file path, filename, page number, content type).
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, RETRIEVE bucket; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, RETRIEVE -> attribute-filtered retrieval ('List all <docs matching attribute> in my knowledge base.'; 'Get the full extracted contents of <doc>.pdf.'; 'What's on page 14 of <doc>.pdf?').

success_criteria.quality: operational pass (NOT RAGAS) — the attribute filter is applied AT RETRIEVAL via a real predicate, not post-hoc LLM filtering. Two distinct mechanisms, both grounded in the shipped CLI + references/query.md: (a) CONTENT-TYPE filtering uses the native `retriever query --content-types text,table,chart,image,infographic` flag (untyped hits excluded at the CLI layer); (b) SOURCE / file-path / filename / PAGE_NUMBER filtering has NO `--where` CLI flag — the canonical mechanism is the LanceDB python predicate one-liner from references/query.md (`.where(...)` / a pandas row predicate over `source_name` and `metadata.page_number`), evaluated against the table BEFORE the limit. PASS = on the labeled filter set, EVERY returned row matches the filter predicate; any off-predicate row in the result = a miss. Binary pass/fail.
success_criteria.time: RETRIEVE — <= 1 min per case (one ingest of a tiny corpus + one filtered query/predicate). Non-agentic.
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'list/get/show only the rows matching <attribute>' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — content-type filter MUST use native `retriever query --content-types ...`; source/page filter MUST use the LanceDB predicate one-liner (no invented `--where` flag on the CLI). Wrong mechanism (e.g. retrieving everything then LLM-filtering, or inventing `--source`/`--page`/`--where` query flags) is a miss.

filtering_mechanisms.content_type: NATIVE CLI flag. `retriever query "<q>" --content-types text,table,chart,image,infographic` (comma-separated; query-time values normalize to canonical hit metadata `type`; untyped hits are excluded). This is a real retrieval-time filter, applied at the CLI layer — not post-hoc.
filtering_mechanisms.source_filename_path: LanceDB predicate one-liner (references/query.md). There is NO `--where`/`--source`/`--filename` flag on `retriever query`. Filter rows where `json.loads(row['source'])['source_name']` equals/ends-with the target filename, evaluated against the table directly (a `.where(...)` SQL predicate or the equivalent pandas row predicate) — BEFORE any top-k limit.
filtering_mechanisms.page_number: LanceDB predicate one-liner (references/query.md). No `--page` CLI flag. Filter rows where `json.loads(row['metadata'])['page_number'] == N` (page_number is a 1-indexed int; it also lives in metadata).
filtering_mechanisms.combined: Compose predicates with `and` in the same LanceDB one-liner (e.g. `source_name == 'woods_frost.pdf' and page_number == 2`), OR ingest a single doc into its own `--table-name` so a query against that table is implicitly source-scoped.
filtering_mechanisms.row_schema: Each LanceDB row written by `retriever ingest` carries: `text`, `source` (JSON string -> `source_name`, full path; basename = filename), `page_number` (1-indexed int), `metadata` (JSON string -> `type` = the content-type {text|table|chart|image|infographic}, `page_number`, `bbox`, ...), and the embedding vector. Source/page predicates read `source`/`metadata`; the CLI `--content-types` flag reads `metadata.type`.

extraction_defaults_from_dry_run.note: Tables/charts/page-elements are ON by default, so a table/chart row is produced for the filter to select. Rungs whose answer is a specific table CELL pass `--use-table-structure` (nemotron-table-structure-v1) so the cell is materialized as a content_type=table row; rungs that only need to PROVE a table-typed row exists do not strictly require it.

environment.ingest_success_line: Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>.
environment.query_hit_shape: CLI `retriever query` returns a JSON array; each hit has exactly {source, page_number, text}; page_number is a 1-indexed int. The LanceDB predicate one-liner reads the underlying table (where `source`/`metadata` are JSON strings) and prints the matching rows' text.

cases.title: Content-type filter: --content-types table -> only table-typed hits
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the content-type leg of the filter task in its simplest form: filter results to ONE content type via the NATIVE `--content-types table` flag, so only table-derived rows come back. Rung 1 floor — single native flag, single content type. (Mechanism: NATIVE `--content-types`, NOT a lancedb predicate.)
cases.prompt: I've loaded data/multimodal_test.pdf and data/table_test.pdf into my knowledge base. List only the TABLE content from them — I want results filtered to tables, not prose.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "animals activities table; yearly values per person" --top-k 10 --content-types table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 2 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (N >= 1; rows span text + table + chart types)
expected_output.filter_mechanism: NATIVE `--content-types table` (no lancedb predicate needed for content-type filtering)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; each hit {source, page_number, text}; EVERY returned hit is a table-typed row (metadata.type == table); text rows excluded
expected_output.answer: Returned rows are the tables only: Table 1 (animals/activities, multimodal_test.pdf p1) and the Year x {Bill,Amy,James,Ted,Susan} grid (table_test.pdf p1). No prose/text-typed rows.

pass_when: query uses the NATIVE `--content-types table` flag (not a lancedb predicate, not post-hoc LLM filtering)
EVERY returned hit is a table-typed row; no text/prose row appears in the result
the table content from BOTH ingested PDFs is selectable (multimodal_test.pdf p1 table + table_test.pdf p1 grid)
off-type rows in the result = miss

cases.title: Content-type filter: text vs chart -> swapping the content-type value changes the row set
cases.complexity_axis: Still the NATIVE `--content-types` leg, but adds over rung 1: two contrasting filter values on the SAME corpus prove the filter is selective, not cosmetic — `--content-types text` returns only prose, `--content-types chart` returns only the chart-typed row, and the sets are disjoint. New dimension: the content-type filter discriminates between modalities on one doc. (Mechanism: NATIVE `--content-types`.)
cases.prompt: From data/multimodal_test.pdf: first give me only the TEXT/prose passages (filter out tables and charts), then separately give me only the CHART content. Keep the two result sets separate.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "introduction sections bullet points placeholder text" --top-k 10 --content-types text
$RETRIEVER query "chart average frequency ranges for speaker drivers; gadgets and costs" --top-k 10 --content-types chart

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (text + table + chart rows present)
expected_output.filter_mechanism: NATIVE `--content-types`, two contrasting values (text vs chart) on one corpus
expected_output.answer: text query returns prose only (Introduction p1, Section One/Two + 3 bullet points p2, Conclusion p3); chart query returns chart-typed rows only (Chart 1 'gadgets and (fictitious) costs' p2-region; Chart 2 'average frequency ranges for speaker drivers' p3). The two result sets are disjoint.
expected_output.disjointness_assertion: no row appears in both the --content-types text set and the --content-types chart set

pass_when: both queries use the NATIVE `--content-types` flag with the right value (text, then chart)
the text query returns ONLY text-typed rows; the chart query returns ONLY chart-typed rows
the two result sets are disjoint (proves the filter is selective, not cosmetic)
no post-hoc LLM filtering substituted for the native flag

cases.title: Filter by SOURCE: only one document's rows via the LanceDB predicate one-liner
cases.complexity_axis: Switches mechanism: source/filename filtering is NOT a CLI flag, so this rung uses the LanceDB predicate one-liner from references/query.md to return rows where source_name == 'woods_frost.pdf' only — the 'Get the full extracted contents of <doc>.pdf' seed query. Adds over rung 2: a multi-doc corpus where the filter must isolate ONE source's rows, applied as a predicate BEFORE the limit (not post-hoc). (Mechanism: LANCEDB PREDICATE, not `--content-types`; there is no `--source`/`--where` CLI flag.)
cases.prompt: My knowledge base has data/multimodal_test.pdf and data/woods_frost.pdf in it. Get me the full extracted contents of woods_frost.pdf ONLY — nothing from the other document.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER_VENV/bin/python -c "import lancedb,json; df=lancedb.connect('./lancedb').open_table('nemo-retriever').to_pandas(); [print(json.loads(r['metadata']).get('page_number'), r['text'][:120]) for _,r in df.iterrows() if json.loads(r['source'])['source_name'].endswith('woods_frost.pdf')]"

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 2 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (rows from BOTH docs)
expected_output.filter_mechanism: LANCEDB PREDICATE one-liner filtering on source_name == woods_frost.pdf (no --source/--where CLI flag exists); predicate applied to the table BEFORE any limit
expected_output.answer: Returns ONLY woods_frost.pdf rows: p1 = the Robert Frost poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' ('His house is in the village', 'miles to go before I sleep'); p2 = Frost's Collections table (A Boy's Will 1913 ... New Hampshire 1923 ... In the Clearing 1962). Zero rows from multimodal_test.pdf.
expected_output.source_assertion: every returned row's source_name ends with 'woods_frost.pdf'; no multimodal_test.pdf row appears

pass_when: source filtering uses the LanceDB predicate one-liner (source_name match), NOT a CLI flag and NOT post-hoc LLM filtering
EVERY returned row's source is woods_frost.pdf; ZERO rows from multimodal_test.pdf
the returned rows cover woods_frost.pdf's content (poem p1 + collections table p2)
any multimodal_test.pdf (off-predicate) row in the result = miss
cases.known_caveat: `retriever query` has no source/filename filter flag; the only retrieval-time source filter is the LanceDB predicate (or ingesting the doc into its own --table-name). Retrieving everything and asking the LLM to keep only woods_frost rows is post-hoc filtering and fails this rung.

cases.title: Filter by PAGE_NUMBER: 'what's on page 2 of <doc>' via the predicate -> only that page's rows
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the PAGE_NUMBER attribute over rung 3's SOURCE attribute: the 'What's on page N of <doc>.pdf?' seed query. The predicate now keys on metadata.page_number == 2 (1-indexed) for a single doc, returning only that page's rows. New dimension: page-level granularity within a source. (Mechanism: LANCEDB PREDICATE on page_number; no `--page` CLI flag.)
cases.prompt: What's on page 2 of data/woods_frost.pdf? Give me only the content from that page.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER_VENV/bin/python -c "import lancedb,json; df=lancedb.connect('./lancedb').open_table('nemo-retriever').to_pandas(); print('\n'.join(r['text'] for _,r in df.iterrows() if json.loads(r['metadata']).get('page_number')==2))"

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (rows on p1 and p2)
expected_output.filter_mechanism: LANCEDB PREDICATE one-liner filtering on metadata.page_number == 2 (1-indexed int; no --page CLI flag exists)
expected_output.answer: Page 2 of woods_frost.pdf holds Frost's Collections table: Collection x Year — A Boy's Will 1913, North of Boston 1914, Mountain Interval 1916, New Hampshire 1923, West Running Brook 1928, A Further Range 1937, A Witness Tree 1942, In the Clearing 1962, Steeple Bush 1947, An Afterword (unknown). The page-1 poem text is NOT returned.
expected_output.page_assertion: every returned row has page_number == 2; no page-1 (poem) row appears

pass_when: page filtering uses the LanceDB predicate one-liner on page_number == 2, NOT a CLI flag and NOT post-hoc filtering
EVERY returned row is from page 2; ZERO page-1 rows
page_number is treated as a 1-indexed int (page 2 = the second page, the collections table)
answer reflects page-2 content (the Frost collections table, e.g. New Hampshire 1923), not the page-1 poem

cases.title: Acceptance: combined SOURCE + PAGE_NUMBER predicate over a multi-doc corpus -> ONLY matching rows
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the filter row. Adds over rung 4: a 3-doc corpus AND a COMPOUND predicate (source_name == X AND page_number == N) so the filter must satisfy two attributes simultaneously and exclude every off-predicate row from the other two docs and the other pages. Asserts the filter is a real predicate applied BEFORE the limit, returning ONLY matching rows. This is the gate the others build to. (Mechanism: combined LANCEDB PREDICATE; native --content-types optionally composes for a third attribute.)
cases.prompt: My knowledge base has data/multimodal_test.pdf, data/woods_frost.pdf, and data/table_test.pdf. Show me ONLY what's on page 1 of woods_frost.pdf — nothing from page 2 of that doc, and nothing from the other two documents.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER_VENV/bin/python -c "import lancedb,json; df=lancedb.connect('./lancedb').open_table('nemo-retriever').to_pandas(); [print(r['text'][:160]) for _,r in df.iterrows() if json.loads(r['source'])['source_name'].endswith('woods_frost.pdf') and json.loads(r['metadata']).get('page_number')==1]"

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (rows from all 3 docs, woods_frost on p1+p2)
expected_output.filter_mechanism: COMPOUND LANCEDB PREDICATE: source_name endswith 'woods_frost.pdf' AND metadata.page_number == 1, applied to the table BEFORE the limit (a real `.where`-style predicate, not post-hoc LLM filtering)
expected_output.answer: Returns ONLY woods_frost.pdf page-1 rows: the Robert Frost poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' ('Whose woods these are I think I know', 'His house is in the village', 'And miles to go before I sleep'). Excludes woods_frost p2 (the collections table), and excludes every multimodal_test.pdf and table_test.pdf row.
expected_output.predicate_assertion: result set == {rows where source_name endswith woods_frost.pdf AND page_number == 1}; any row violating either clause (wrong doc OR wrong page) in the result = a miss

pass_when: the combined source+page filter is a real predicate (source_name == woods_frost.pdf AND page_number == 1) applied at retrieval, BEFORE any limit — not post-hoc LLM filtering
EVERY returned row satisfies BOTH clauses; ZERO off-predicate rows (no woods_frost p2, no multimodal_test.pdf, no table_test.pdf)
the returned page-1 content is the Frost poem (not the page-2 collections table)
any single off-predicate row (wrong document OR wrong page) in the result fails the acceptance gate
